subject_name: DAILY MAIL AND GENERAL TRUST P L C
summary: DAILY MAIL AND GENERAL TRUST P L C (LEI: 4OFD47D73QFJ1T1MOF29; GB Companies House: 00184594) is identifier-confirmed and registered in the United Kingdom. Per UK Companies House, the 4th Viscount Rothermere (The Right Honourable Jonathan Harold Eamond Vere) currently holds 75% or more of voting rights via control over the trustees of a trust, and is also a director since 1995; Mrs Aine Deirdre O'Reilly currently holds 75% or more of voting rights directly, with Nigel Timothy Bentley, Thomas Stuart Gillespie, and Francois Leo Morin also recorded as current holders of 75%-or-more voting-right interests, suggesting these are co-trustees or nominees within the same trust arrangement. GLEIF data records a GLEIF reporting exception at the parent level — the parent entity does not consolidate the subject — leaving the ultimate parent unidentified, while the subject itself consolidates three direct subsidiaries: TREPP UK LIMITED, DMGB LIMITED, and DAILY MAIL AND GENERAL HOLDINGS LIMITED. Four structural risk signals were identified: the ownership chain includes a trust or non-corporate arrangement (high confidence, per AMLA CDD RTS condition (a)); the chain reaches a jurisdiction outside the EU/EEA (high confidence, per AMLA CDD RTS condition (b)); the chain has three or more corporate layers meeting the AMLA complexity threshold (medium confidence); and these factors combine into a complex corporate structure finding (high confidence) — an additional low-confidence advisory signal notes that the combination of opacity and layering warrants confirmation of a legitimate economic rationale before reliance. No gaps were recorded in the evidence packet.

risks.label: Opaque ownership (super-secure)
risks.rationale: Ownership chain contains: anonymous entity in ownership chain.

risks.label: Trust / arrangement
risks.rationale: Ownership chain includes a trust or non-corporate arrangement (1 entity statement(s)). AMLA CDD RTS condition (a).

risks.label: Non-EU jurisdiction
risks.rationale: Ownership chain reaches into jurisdictions outside the EU/EEA: GB. AMLA CDD RTS condition (b).

risks.label: Complex ownership layers
risks.rationale: Ownership chain has 3 corporate layers (AMLA threshold: ≥3).

risks.label: Complex corporate structure
risks.rationale: Meets AMLA CDD RTS threshold for a complex corporate structure: 3 layers of ownership combined with trust/arrangement, non-EU jurisdiction.

risks.label: Possible Obfuscation
risks.rationale: Advisory: structure combines opacity (unknown/anonymous parties) with complex layering. AMLA CDD RTS subjective condition — confirm whether there is a legitimate economic rationale before relying on this signal.
